purpose: The commit fixes a bug where hci_cmd_sync_queue was returning success even when HCI_UNREGISTER flag had been set. This flag is set after hci_unregister_dev has been called, which means the hdev will be freed. The function now returns an error if this flag is set.
The commit is removing an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c. This file already includes linux/tty.h which pulls linux/termios.h and does not need the stray include.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' in perf-c2c tool. It seems to be related to cache performance analysis.
The commit fixes a bug in the hugetlb module of the Linux kernel that can lead to memory leaks or corruption. It also introduces a new feature for naming private anonymous VMAs.

The commit adds two new properties to a YAML file related to the Realtek rt5682s codec. These properties are AVDD-supply and MICVDD-supply which represent the power supplies for the analog and microphone parts of the codec respectively.

The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call. The leak was caused by not freeing resources in the error handling path of the probe that were released in the remove function.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in C programming language.

The commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed in cfg80211_auth_request. The change is necessary because the key_idx can be -1, which would otherwise lead to a warning from static checkers such as smatch.
The commit is about sorting CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs files. It's a minor change that doesn't affect the functionality of the code.

The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode in the MCP251XFD driver. This function is used throughout the codebase to determine if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
The commit is about skipping tests when Real Time Clock (RTC) is not present. It seems to be a performance enhancement as it avoids unnecessary failures.

The commit removes an unnecessary assignment to a variable named 'bytes'. The variable is assigned a value that is never read in the following if block. This change cleans up a clang scan build warning.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks. It also introduces a new feature to allow rcuscale without RCU Tasks.
The commit is about removing duplicated 'be split' in a file named spte.h. It seems to be related to memory management or virtualization.
The commit reverts a change that was made to populate a clock table based on DCFCLK states. The purpose of this change is unclear without context.
The commit adds a new compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC (a.k.a R9A07G054) to the Mali-Bifrost GPU in the devicetree bindings.

The commit changes various files related to PCIe drivers in Linux kernel. It appears to be a refactoring effort to replace the old 'pcie_port' structure with the new 'dw_pcie_rp' structure. This change is likely part of an ongoing effort to modernize and streamline the design of these drivers.
The commit fixes a clang warning about format specifier mismatch between unsigned short (u16) and int. It changes the format specifier from %#06hx to %#06x.
The commit fixes a warning in MIPS architecture related to branch range check. It changes the comparison from unsigned to signed, which is necessary because the offset can be negative.
The commit removes a feature from the driver that was used for power saving in certain scenarios. It also simplifies the code by removing an unnecessary switch case.
The commit declares a symbol as static in arm64/kernel/cpu_errata.c file. This is done to avoid potential issues with sparse warnings.
The commit removes an incorrect interrupt flags cell from a device tree binding for MDSS nodes in the display subsystem.
The commit introduces a new feature to allocate arrays in ctrl_ref struct. It also modifies existing code to handle the allocation of array space for these structures.
The commit adds support for new hardware platforms to the socinfo driver in the Linux kernel. It specifically mentions adding support for SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P and one more SM8450.
The commit recognizes all possible chip cuts from A to P instead of printing 'unknown' when it is greater than E.

The commit removes a field from a struct in C that is used to keep track of whether a private anonymous memory region (VMA) is enabled. This change seems to be related to improving the management and tracking of these regions.

This commit appears to be a refactoring of code related to memory management in the RISC-V architecture. It moves the pt_ops macro to adjacent #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL and groups first type usage cases into one.
The commit introduces new hardware configurations for GPI DMA and QUP in a device tree file. It seems to enable these features for future use.
This commit fixes a typo in comments and replaces an unclear comment with one that better explains what is being captured.
The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function of igc driver for Intel i225 chipset. The infinite loop occurs when the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired.
The commit introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel for managing private anonymous VMAs.
The commit removes a pointless hack in the NFS file system code. The hack was checking if a file is a swap file or not, which is always defined as false because IS_SWAPFILE is always undefined.

The commit introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel that allows for naming private anonymous VMAs (Virtual Memory Areas). This is done through an interface provided by prctl and /proc reporting. The addition of this feature improves observability and memory accounting in multi-allocator environments.
The commit replaces 'next' version of cpumask functions with 'first' where appropriate. It also adds a new function to get the first unset CPU in a cpumask.
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR in brcmfmac driver. This is done to prevent the host driver from trying to get TIM information from firmware after roaming.
The commit adds support for a platform without an amplifier to the Intel SoC (System on Chip) codebase. It appears to be related to audio processing.
The commit introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel that fails zerocopy sendmsg requests for protocols that don't support it. It also adds a check to see if the socket supports zero-copy sends.

The commit introduces a new feature to improve the handling of CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) characters in the documentation. It checks if the xeCJK package is installed before loading it, which helps avoid errors when using Asian fonts.

The commit is about allowing 'case' macros in scripts/checkpatch.pl without enclosing them in parentheses. This change seems to be related to the checkpatch script used for checking Linux kernel patches.

The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with regular atomic helpers in a driver for simple-framebuffer platform devices. It seems to be an architectural improvement and does not directly fix any bugs or introduce new features.

The commit introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel, specifically for managing private anonymous VMAs in the media subsystem. It adds an interface for jpeg decoding workers and ensures that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.

The commit message suggests that it is related to improving memory management in the Linux kernel. It mentions a feature for naming private anonymous VMAs, which could be used for debugging and performance analysis.

This commit introduces a new feature to manage private anonymous VMAs in the Linux kernel. It adds a field to /proc/pid/maps and /proc/pid/smaps to show a userspace-provided name for anonymous memory regions.

The commit fixes a CFI (Control Flow Integrity) violation in the AMDGPU driver by updating the function pointer prototype of hqd_destroy() callback. The violation was caused by an incorrect type of reset_type parameter.

The commit corrects a typo in the 'stm32-pinctrl.yaml' file. It changes the property name from 'reset' to 'resets'. This change is necessary because the actual users are correct and it didn't show up due to missing 'additionalProperties: false'. The commit also adds a missing 'additionalProperties'.
The commit is about silencing a GCC warning related to array bounds. It modifies the Makefile of the ath9k driver to silence this specific warning for non-W=1+ builds.
The commit changes the way power management is handled in a specific driver for an Allwinner device. It replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get.

The commit splits out fixed file installation and removal into a separate function from the original one in 'io_uring/filetable.c'. It also introduces a new function '__io_fixed_fd_install' that takes an additional parameter 'ctx' of type 'struct io_ring_ctx *' instead of 'struct io_kiocb *'. This change is likely to improve the organization and readability of the code.

The commit introduces a new feature to the Linux kernel that allows for better management of private anonymous VMAs (Virtual Memory Areas). It adds a field to /proc/pid/maps and /proc/pid/smaps to show a userspace-provided name for anonymous VMAs, displayed as [anon:<name>]. Userspace can set the name for a region of memory by calling prctl(PR_SET_VMA, PR_SET_VMA_ANON_NAME, start, len, name).
The commit removes a macro definition that is used only once in another part of the code. The purpose seems to be to clean up the code by removing unnecessary macros.
